mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return probability during rate declines falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, severe labor market slack constrains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which amplifies earnings revisions and produces positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return probability above 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and operational leverage, which translates to positive short-term equity momentum when the economy is in a confirmed expansion phase.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms aggregate demand resilience, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation increases combined with stagnant policy rates trigger expectations of aggressive future tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: High inflation with low rates fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with hit rate below 45% across tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls additions signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and yields positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payrolls exceeding 150k are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of observations, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which consistently supports positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield a positive return probability below 50% across all tested sub-periods, indicating no predictive power.
